proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of feature_078 (continuous, range 30-90, mean 65) are associated with longer pfs_months (positive linear association).

analyses.code: import statsmodels.formula.api as smf; smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Univariate OLS: feature_078 has a strong positive linear association with pfs_months. beta = +0.1765 months per unit, p < 1e-300. Pearson r = +0.766. Mean PFS rises monotonically from 1.33 mo in the lowest decile (<=52.3) to 7.45 mo in the highest decile (>77.8). This is the single strongest predictor in the dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher levels of the 3-level ordinal feature_057 (values 0, 1, 2) are associated with shorter pfs_months (negative ordinal association consistent with a performance-status-like grade).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_057', data=df).fit(); df.groupby('feature_057')['pfs_months'].mean()
analyses.result_summary: Strong negative ordinal effect: mean PFS = 5.27 (level 0, n=17592), 4.05 (level 1, n=24971), 2.91 (level 2, n=7437). Linear regression beta = -1.190 months per level, p < 1e-300. Spearman rho = -0.36.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_051 = 1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_051 = 0 (negative effect of this binary indicator, prevalence 55%).

analyses.code: from scipy import stats; stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_051']==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_051']==0,'pfs_months'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS = 3.70 mo when feature_051 = 1 (n=27481) vs 5.06 mo when feature_051 = 0 (n=22519). Welch t-test p < 1e-300. Difference = -1.352 months. The largest binary main-effect signal in the dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_038 = 1 (prevalence 20%) have longer pfs_months than patients with feature_038 = 0 (positive main effect; candidate beneficial therapy or favorable biomarker).

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_038']==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_038']==0,'pfs_months'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS = 5.09 mo when feature_038 = 1 (n=10022) vs 4.12 mo when feature_038 = 0 (n=39978). Difference = +0.972 months, p = 1.7e-216. The largest positive binary main-effect signal in the dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013 = 1 (prevalence 42%) is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_013 = 0 (negative main effect).
feature_043 = 1 (prevalence 35%) is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_043 = 0 (negative main effect).

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_013']==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_013']==0,'pfs_months'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS = 4.13 mo when feature_013 = 1 (n=20898) vs 4.45 mo when feature_013 = 0 (n=29102). Difference = -0.327 months, p = 1.9e-58.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_043']==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_043']==0,'pfs_months'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS = 4.13 mo when feature_043 = 1 (n=17657) vs 4.43 mo when feature_043 = 0 (n=32343). Difference = -0.305 months, p = 1.0e-49.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_092 (continuous, range 1.5-5.5, mean 3.80, possibly serum albumin) is associated with longer pfs_months (positive linear association).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_092', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Univariate OLS: feature_092 beta = +0.4654 months per unit, p = 3.2e-115. Pearson r = +0.102. After multivariable adjustment for feature_078, feature_057, feature_051, feature_038, etc., the effect becomes +0.470 per unit (p < 1e-300), suggesting a robust independent positive effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_099 (continuous, range 0-24.6, mean 3.84) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative association consistent with an inflammatory or tumor-burden marker).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_099', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Univariate OLS: feature_099 beta = -0.0730 months per unit, p = 2.2e-167. Pearson r = -0.123. The negative association persists in the multivariable model (beta = -0.076 per unit).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_109 = 1 (rare, prevalence 4.5%) is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_109 = 0.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_109']==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_109']==0,'pfs_months'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS = 4.09 mo when feature_109 = 1 (n=2272) vs 4.32 mo when feature_109 = 0 (n=47728). Difference = -0.228 months, p = 3.5e-7. Modest main effect but, as shown later, large effect modifier of feature_038.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_067 = 1 (rare, prevalence 3.0%) is associated with longer pfs_months than feature_067 = 0.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_067']==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_067']==0,'pfs_months'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS = 4.51 mo when feature_067 = 1 (n=1506) vs 4.30 mo when feature_067 = 0 (n=48494). Difference = +0.221 months, p = 7.3e-4. Modest main effect; later analyses show feature_067 augments the effect of feature_038.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A multivariable OLS model on the top main-effect predictors (feature_078, feature_057, feature_051, feature_038, feature_013, feature_043, feature_099, feature_092, feature_109, feature_067) jointly explains a large share of variance in pfs_months, with each remaining significant after adjustment.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_057 + feature_051 + feature_038 + feature_013 + feature_043 + feature_099 + feature_092 + feature_109 + feature_067', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: R^2 = 0.857. Adjusted coefficients (all p<1e-3 except feature_067 p=0.36): feature_078 +0.176, feature_057 -1.171, feature_051 -1.360, feature_038 +0.943, feature_013 -0.354, feature_043 -0.287, feature_099 -0.076, feature_092 +0.470, feature_109 -0.356. Confirms each remains independently associated, and they jointly explain ~86% of PFS variance.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive effect of feature_038 on pfs_months is attenuated or abolished in patients with feature_013 = 1 (negative interaction between feature_038 and feature_013).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_038 * feature_013 + feature_078 + feature_057 + feature_051 + feature_092 + feature_099 + feature_043', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta(feature_038:feature_013) = -1.634 months, p < 1e-300. Stratified means: feature_038=1 vs 0 yields +1.66 mo when feature_013=0 (5.77 vs 4.12) and effectively 0 mo when feature_013=1 (4.14 vs 4.12). feature_038's benefit disappears entirely when feature_013 = 1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive effect of feature_038 on pfs_months is also attenuated or abolished in patients with feature_043 = 1 (negative interaction between feature_038 and feature_043, separately from the feature_013 interaction).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_038 * feature_043 + feature_038 * feature_013 + feature_078 + feature_057 + feature_051 + feature_092 + feature_099', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: In the joint model, interaction beta(feature_038:feature_043) = -1.473 months, p < 1e-300; beta(feature_038:feature_013) = -1.644, p < 1e-300; main feature_038 beta = +2.147, p < 1e-300. Both feature_013 and feature_043 independently abolish feature_038's benefit, and either being positive is sufficient to negate the effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In the 'double-negative' subgroup (feature_013 = 0 AND feature_043 = 0), feature_038 produces a markedly larger improvement in pfs_months than the overall +0.97-month main effect (subgroup-defined effect heterogeneity).

analyses.code: sub = df[(df['feature_013']==0) & (df['feature_043']==0)]; smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_038 + feature_078 + feature_057 + feature_051 + feature_092 + feature_099', data=sub).fit()
analyses.result_summary: In the double-negative subgroup (n=18853), unadjusted mean PFS = 6.64 (feature_038=1, n=3763) vs 4.12 (feature_038=0, n=15090); difference = +2.520 months, p < 1e-300. Adjusted feature_038 coefficient = +2.516, p < 1e-300. Outside this subgroup, raw differences are: f013=0,f043=1 +0.07 mo (p=0.16); f013=1,f043=0 +0.06 mo (p=0.17); f013=1,f043=1 -0.04 mo (p=0.52). The benefit of feature_038 is essentially confined to double-negative patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients with feature_038 = 1, feature_067 = 1 further increases pfs_months relative to feature_067 = 0 (positive interaction feature_038 x feature_067).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_038 * feature_067 + feature_078 + feature_057 + feature_051 + feature_092 + feature_099 + feature_013 + feature_043', data=df).fit(); within feature_038=1 stratum: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_067', data=df[df['feature_038']==1]).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta(feature_038:feature_067) = +0.888, p = 1.6e-56. Stratified: when feature_038=1, mean PFS = 6.05 vs 5.06 (delta = +1.00 mo, p = 1.2e-9 within stratum); when feature_038=0, mean PFS = 4.13 vs 4.12 (delta ~0). feature_067 confers an additional ~+1 month of PFS only in the presence of feature_038.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients with feature_038 = 1, feature_109 = 1 reverses the benefit of feature_038, producing shorter pfs_months than feature_038 = 1 / feature_109 = 0 (strong negative interaction feature_038 x feature_109).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_038 * feature_109 + feature_078 + feature_057 + feature_051 + feature_092 + feature_099 + feature_013 + feature_043', data=df).fit(); stratified means by (feature_038, feature_109)
analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta(feature_038:feature_109) = -0.987, p = 2.1e-102. Cells: f038=0/f109=0 4.12, f038=0/f109=1 4.13, f038=1/f109=0 5.14, f038=1/f109=1 3.97. So feature_109 has no effect when feature_038=0 but a -1.17 month effect when feature_038=1 (p=3.4e-18 within feature_038=1 stratum), turning a benefit into harm.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013, feature_067, and feature_109 are mutually exclusive categories of a single underlying property: feature_067 = 1 and feature_109 = 1 occur only when feature_013 = 0; this constrains how their effects on pfs_months should be interpreted (different alternative states of the same axis).

analyses.code: pd.crosstab(df['feature_013'], df['feature_067']); pd.crosstab(df['feature_013'], df['feature_109'])
analyses.result_summary: Cross-tabs show perfect exclusivity: when feature_013 = 1, feature_067 = 0 in all 20898 patients and feature_109 = 0 in all 20898 patients. When feature_013 = 0, feature_067 occurs in 1506/29102 (5.2%) and feature_109 in 2272/29102 (7.8%). This is consistent with a categorical molecular axis where feature_013 codes one alteration, and feature_067/feature_109 code two distinct alternative states (or sub-classes) of the same axis. The differential interactions with feature_038 (feature_067 enhances, feature_109 reverses) make biological sense if these are subclasses of a 'wild-type-like' background.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive effect of feature_078 on pfs_months has a nonlinear (concave-up) component: the slope steepens at the upper end of the range, so a quadratic term improves model fit.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + I(feature_078**2) + feature_057 + feature_051 + feature_038 + feature_092 + feature_099 + feature_013 + feature_043', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Quadratic term beta = +0.000676, p = 1.4e-123. Although small per unit, the signal is highly significant across n=50000 and indicates a mild upward curvature: each additional unit of feature_078 is worth more PFS at higher values than at lower values.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The PFS benefit of feature_038 is smaller in patients with worse feature_057 (higher levels), i.e. negative interaction feature_038 x feature_057.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_057 * feature_038 + feature_078 + feature_051 + feature_092 + feature_099 + feature_013 + feature_043', data=df).fit(); stratified feature_038 effect by feature_057
analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta(feature_057:feature_038) = -0.052, p = 3.1e-4. Stratified raw deltas: feature_057=0 +0.93 mo, feature_057=1 +0.99 mo, feature_057=2 +0.90 mo. Statistically significant but very small in magnitude relative to the feature_038 main effect; the benefit of feature_038 is largely preserved across performance-status strata.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The PFS benefit of feature_038 differs by feature_078 (continuous) — i.e., the treatment effect varies across the 'fitness' continuum captured by feature_078.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 * feature_038 + feature_057 + feature_051 + feature_092 + feature_099 + feature_013 + feature_043', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta(feature_078:feature_038) = +0.0012 per unit, p = 0.20. Not significant. The effect of feature_038 does not meaningfully vary along feature_078; the benefit is uniform across this dimension.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Race (feature_064; categories white, black, hispanic, asian, other) is associated with pfs_months (mean differs across categories).
The benefit of feature_038 differs across race categories of feature_064 (effect-modification by race).

analyses.code: stats.f_oneway(...); smf.ols('pfs_months ~ C(feature_064) + feature_078 + feature_057 + feature_051 + feature_038 + feature_092 + feature_099', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Univariate ANOVA: F=0.87, p=0.48. Means: white 4.32, hispanic 4.32, asian 4.31, black 4.27, other 4.41. After multivariable adjustment, no race coefficient is significant (all p > 0.35). No race main effect on pfs_months.

analyses.code: stratified feature_038 effect by race
analyses.result_summary: Stratified: white +0.92 mo, black +0.97, hispanic +1.11, asian +1.09, other +1.22. Differences across races are small and overlap; no evidence the feature_038 benefit differs systematically by race.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Insurance category (feature_018; medicare, private, uninsured, medicaid) is associated with pfs_months.

analyses.code: stats.f_oneway across feature_018 levels; smf.ols('pfs_months ~ C(feature_018) + clinical covariates', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Univariate ANOVA: F=1.48, p=0.22. Means: medicare 4.31, private 4.33, uninsured 4.25, medicaid 4.28. After multivariable adjustment all levels p > 0.16. No detectable insurance effect on pfs_months in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_007 (binary, prevalence 45%) is associated with pfs_months (sex-like demographic indicator).
feature_122 (binary, prevalence 45%) is associated with pfs_months.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_007', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Univariate: feature_007 beta = -0.006 mo, p = 0.77. No association with pfs_months.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_122', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Univariate: difference in mean PFS between feature_122=1 and feature_122=0 is small, t-test p > 0.4. No detectable main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The ordinal/count features feature_071 (range 0-10), feature_086 (binary, 28%), feature_025/feature_026/feature_033/feature_096/feature_075 (5-level ordinals) each show a univariate association with pfs_months.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_071', data=df).fit() (and similarly for the others); spearmanr for ordinals
analyses.result_summary: feature_071 beta = -0.002, p = 0.55. feature_086 t-test p = 0.81. Spearman rho's: feature_025 +0.006 (p=0.15), feature_075 -0.002 (p=0.67), feature_026 -0.006 (p=0.16), feature_096 -0.004 (p=0.33), feature_033 +0.003 (p=0.55). None reaches significance. These features are not associated with pfs_months at the univariate level.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_051 effect on pfs_months is amplified at higher feature_057 levels (interaction feature_051 x feature_057).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_051 * feature_057 + feature_078 + feature_038 + feature_092 + feature_099 + feature_013 + feature_043', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta(feature_051:feature_057) = +0.048, p = 2.9e-5. Direction is opposite to the hypothesis: the feature_051 deficit narrows slightly as feature_057 worsens (i.e., feature_051 has a smaller incremental effect when feature_057 is already poor). Statistically significant but small; mostly consistent with a near-additive joint effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A multivariable model with the top main effects, the feature_078 quadratic term, and the four feature_038 interactions (with feature_013, feature_043, feature_067, feature_109) explains substantially more PFS variance than main effects alone, and crystallizes the interpretation that feature_038 is a treatment whose benefit is biomarker-conditional.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_038 * (feature_013 + feature_043 + feature_067 + feature_109) + feature_078 + I(feature_078**2) + feature_057 + feature_051 + feature_092 + feature_099', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Full model R^2 = 0.898 vs main-effects-only R^2 = 0.857 (delta R^2 = 0.041; F-test highly significant). All four feature_038 interactions remain significant (feature_013 -1.64, feature_043 -1.47, feature_067 +0.89, feature_109 -0.99). Net feature_038 effect: +2.15 mo in patients with feature_013=0 AND feature_043=0 AND feature_067=0 AND feature_109=0; +3.04 mo with feature_067=1; +1.16 with feature_109=1; near zero in feature_013=1 or feature_043=1. The dataset is well-described by a single 'treatment' (feature_038) whose benefit is governed by a biomarker axis (feature_013/feature_043/feature_067/feature_109) plus a small set of prognostic factors (feature_078, feature_057, feature_051, feature_092, feature_099).
